name: Repository
description: Mediates between the domain and data mapping layers using a collection-like interface for accessing domain objects.
problem: Need to abstract data access logic from business logic while providing a clean interface for querying domain objects.
solution: Create a repository class that encapsulates data access logic and exposes domain objects through a collection-like interface.
when_to_use: When you want to abstract persistence details from domain logic
When you have complex querying requirements
When you need to test business logic without database
When you might switch data stores
when_not_to_use: For simple CRUD applications with minimal business logic
When ORM already provides sufficient abstraction

participants.name: Repository Interface
participants.role: Defines the contract for data access

participants.name: Concrete Repository
participants.role: Implements data access using specific data store

participants.name: Data Mapper/ORM
participants.role: Maps between domain objects and data store

cons: Can become bloated with many query methods
